sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Measure how LLM instruction-following accuracy degrades as the number of simultaneous constraints in a prompt increases.

sections.heading: Motivation
context: This project addresses a gap in NLP & Language Models research.
Measure how LLM instruction-following accuracy degrades as the number of simultaneous constraints in a prompt increases.
Current approaches lack systematic evaluation under realistic constraints.
Practitioners need quantitative guidance for deployment decisions.
impact: Provides reproducible baselines for future comparison.
Establishes best practices for evaluation methodology.
Creates reusable tooling for the research community.

items.text: How does the proposed approach compare to existing baselines on standard nlp benchmarks?
What are the key factors driving performance differences?
How sensitive is the method to hyperparameter choices and data distribution?
What are the computational trade-offs compared to simpler approaches?

items.label: Robustness
items.value: Performance under distribution shift and adversarial perturbation.

items.value: Bootstrap 95% CI with Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons.

items.mitigation: Early data audit in Week 1; fallback to synthetic data.

items.risk: Baselines underperform due to implementation bugs
items.mitigation: Use official implementations where available; verify against published numbers.

items.risk: Compute budget insufficient for full grid
items.mitigation: Run dev split first; prioritize 2 most important conditions.
Increase sample size; use paired tests; report effect sizes.
